basics.name: Alexandre Vanryssel
basics.label: Freelance Tech Lead, Java, JS & Web3
basics.summary: Tech Lead with over 15 years of experience, specializing in Java and Spring Boot, distributed architectures, and mission-critical products for major accounts (retail, finance). Technical team leadership, recruitment, architecture design, and support for quality, security, observability, and operations policies. Additional expertise in DevOps and cloud (Docker, Kubernetes, GCP, Azure, CI/CD) and blockchain (Ethereum, Tezos, Avalanche, Sui, smart contracts).

work.name: Adeo
work.summary: Technical leadership of a team of around 10 people across several software products in the costing domain (retail). Adeo via Astek (until Feb. 2025), then freelance.
highlights: Architecture and design; development; CI/CD pipelines and operations
Rollout of cross-cutting policies: quality, security, observability, and production reliability
Technical leadership and alignment with business and cross-functional stakeholders
Recruitment and onboarding of new team members
Technical environment: Java, Spring, Spring Boot, MongoDB, PostgreSQL, Neo4J, Angular, Vue.js, Kafka, Docker, Kubernetes, GCP, Github, Github Actions, Github Copilot, Gatling, Datadog

work.name: Marigold
work.summary: Management of the team responsible for Ligo, the Tezos blockchain smart contract language.
highlights: Management of a 12-person team distributed internationally
Tracking language evolution
Implementation of a library registry for the developer ecosystem

work.name: Ineat Lab (Ineat Group), then Innovation Lab (Astek)
work.summary: Blockchain and AI referent: technology watch, prototypes, training, and support for RFP responses.
highlights: Blockchain: POCs and case studies, technology watch, internal training, RFP responses, articles and meetups/conferences
Teaching: supervision of blockchain practical labs at Supinfo
AI: RAG study to synthesize processing errors from a business system
GraphRAG POC: knowledge graph to improve search accuracy in a legal use case
Model benchmarking (Gemini, GPT, Llama, DeepSeek, Claude, Qwen, etc.)

Technical environment: React, JavaScript, TypeScript, smart contracts, Ethereum (Solidity, Truffle, Remix, Web3.js), Tezos (Ligo, Taquito), Python, Jupyter, Ollama, OpenWebUI, Knowledge Graph, vectorization and embeddings, Atlas Vector Search, PGVector, Neo4J

work.name: Norauto (via Ineat Group)
work.summary: Tech Lead for the Concept Store team (Norauto): design, industrialization, and delivery of new store concepts (POC to product).
highlights: Architecture design and technical choices
API development and development support
Delivery management and coordination with external partners
Technical environment: Java, Spring Boot, MongoDB, PostgreSQL, Azure, Gitlab, Gitlab CI

work.name: Leroy Merlin (via Ineat Group)
work.summary: Scrum Master for a 5-person team responsible for the delivery promise on the Leroymerlin.fr marketplace.
highlights: Agile facilitation for the team
Cross-team synchronization
Participation in design and architecture
Technical environment: Java, Spring Boot, MongoDB, Kafka, Docker, Kubernetes, GCP, Github, Github Actions

work.name: Adeo (via Ineat Group)
work.summary: Agile coaching for 4 Adeo teams: Scrum / Kanban introduction and practice stabilization.

work.name: Oney Bank (via Ineat Group)
work.summary: Integration of a mobile payment service and implementation of strong customer authentication.
highlights: Scrum implementation and team facilitation
Preliminary studies, technical and functional design, POCs
Contribution to architecture decisions
Technical support on regulatory and legal topics related to payment flows
Technical environment: Java, Spring Boot

work.name: Leroy Merlin (via Ineat Group)
work.summary: Coordination of a sales application prototype, then leadership of the iOS and Android application redesign.
highlights: Hands-on management of 6 to 10 people
Technical scoping, design, and coordination with feature teams
User workshops and experimentation (Lean Startup)
Planning, deliverables, and budget tracking; vendor management
Technical environment: Android, iOS, Java

work.name: Humanis (via Ineat Group)
work.summary: Instance planning application for the Humanis group — project management and Java development.
highlights: Management of a small team; project management and budget tracking
Coordination between business analysts, project management, and business stakeholders
Java design and development
Technical environment: Java, Hibernate, Struts, JSP, Oracle, PostgreSQL, Tomcat

work.name: Sopra Group
work.summary: Projects for various retail sector clients.
highlights: Java/JEE development
Technical environment: Java, Hibernate, Struts, JSP, Oracle, PostgreSQL, Websphere, Tomcat

certificates.name: Google Cloud Platform Fundamentals: Core Infrastructure

projects.name: Instampy.fr
projects.description: Founder & architect — B2C SaaS for digital timestamping and proof of anteriority (EURL Blue Mossa). End-to-end product delivery (architecture through production).
highlights: Cloud-agnostic full-stack architecture with clear separation of concerns and anti-vendor lock-in abstraction layers
Modular multi-chain blockchain architecture (Sui + Avalanche, extensible), custom smart contracts (Move + Solidity), gas fee security
SSR frontend, REST API, relational database, FR/EN multilingual UI, online payments, GDPR compliance
Multi-environment deployment on Railway, GitHub Actions CI/CD, uptime monitoring, DNS, DMARC/DKIM
Technical environment: Nuxt 4, Vue 3, TypeScript, Node.js, Express, PostgreSQL, Prisma, Supabase, Railway, Sui, Avalanche, Move, Solidity, Stripe

projects.name: Solving Cain's Jawbone
projects.description: Development of an AI-based tool to help solve Cain's Jawbone, a complex literary puzzle.
highlights: Definition of entities and relationships in the French and English texts of the work
Prompt engineering for data extraction and structuring
Use of vector databases for storage and search
Creation of a knowledge graph to improve vector search relevance
Technical environment: Python, Jupyter, Ollama, OpenWebUI, Neo4J, RAG, GraphRAG, Cursor, Model Context Protocols

projects.name: Tezocracy
projects.description: Online tool to help Tezos bakers vote on governance proposals.
highlights: Development in collaboration with MIDL.dev
Technical environment: React, Taquito, Tezos, GitHub Actions, Netlify

projects.name: Tezos testnet faucet
projects.description: Faucet for Tezos testnets, distributing Tez tokens to Tezos addresses.
highlights: Initially a personal project
Funded by the Tezos Foundation to integrate it as the official faucet
Handover to OxheadAlpha and maintenance, before transfer to the foundation
Technical environment: React, Taquito, Tezos, GitHub Actions, Netlify
